name: Qatar
overview: Qatar was the first GCC state with a standalone data protection law — Law No. 13 of 2016 on Personal Data Privacy Protection — enforced by the National Cyber Governance and Assurance Affairs (NCSA/NPC framework) with guidelines issued in 2020-2021. The Qatar Financial Centre (QFC) operates its own GDPR-aligned Data Protection Regulations 2021 with a dedicated Data Protection Office.

regulator.name: National Cyber Security Agency (NCSA) — Compliance and Data Protection; QFC Data Protection Office (QFC)

legal_instruments.title: Law No. 13 of 2016 on Personal Data Privacy Protection (PDPPL)
legal_instruments.summary: Consent-based framework with transparency and security obligations, special protections ('data of special nature' including children's and health data requiring permits), breach notification, and fines up to QAR 5 million.

legal_instruments.title: QFC Data Protection Regulations 2021
legal_instruments.summary: GDPR-aligned regime for QFC-registered entities: lawful bases, DPIAs, breach notification (72 hours), adequacy-based transfer rules and fines up to USD 1.5 million.

developments.title: NCSA consolidates data protection oversight
developments.summary: Data protection supervision continued under the National Cyber Security Agency's compliance arm, with updated PDPPL guidance for controllers and processors.

developments.title: QFC DPO issues first enforcement guidance cycle
developments.summary: The QFC Data Protection Office matured its supervision under the 2021 Regulations, including breach-reporting expectations for financial firms.

penalties: PDPPL: fines up to QAR 5 million for violations such as processing special-nature data without a permit or failing breach notification. QFC: fines up to USD 1.5 million.
cross_border: PDPPL requires protections to travel with the data (no formal adequacy list at state level). QFC regime uses adequacy list plus safeguards for transfers out of the QFC.
